title: Human Fracture Analysis Canon Light

architectural_position.operational_purpose: To analyze human narratives in rupture contexts and identify structural signals, risks, and dignity violations.

working_definition: Human Fracture Analysis is a structural diagnostic canon for detecting, interpreting, and reducing dehumanization, avoidable harm, and dignity failure in intimate and family rupture processes.

design_logic.runtime_intent: Narrative input is reduced to observable structural signals, evaluated through constraint logic, and rendered as explainable structural assessments.

axioms.name: Freedom Without Captivity
axioms.statement: No intimate bond is ethically valid if maintained by coercion, guilt pressure, surveillance, fear, or financial captivity.

axioms.name: Dignity After Love
axioms.statement: When love ends, the human dignity of both parties must remain protected.

axioms.name: Pain Is Not a License
axioms.statement: Pain, humiliation, and grief do not create moral permission for revenge, degradation, or bitterness as identity.

axioms.name: Children Remain Center
axioms.statement: Children remain a continuing moral center after the romantic bond breaks.

axioms.name: Self-Accountability Without Self-Erasure
axioms.statement: One must examine one's own contribution to the rupture without collapsing into self-annihilation.

axioms.name: No Secondary War Zone
axioms.statement: A separation must not be converted into a secondary war zone through humiliation, threats, strategic cruelty, or moral theater.

axioms.name: Reality-Bound Ethics
axioms.statement: Structural evaluation in family rupture must include concrete realities such as money, housing, logistics, work capacity, and downstream consequences.

axioms.name: Vulnerability Is Part of the Field
axioms.statement: Breakdown, panic, shame, grief, and temporary loss of function are part of the structural field and must be interpreted with seriousness.

axioms.name: Residual Goodwill Matters
axioms.statement: Even when romantic love dies, care, mutual easing, and goodwill may remain ethically meaningful.

axioms.name: Questions Have Ethical Boundaries
axioms.statement: Not every true question is an ethical question to ask. Some questions degrade rather than clarify.

axioms.name: Belonging Continues After Rupture
axioms.statement: Family rupture does not erase all belonging. Bonds of responsibility and symbolic membership may continue.

axioms.name: A Dignified Ending Minimizes Secondary Damage
axioms.statement: The most ethical separation is not the least painful one, but the one that minimizes avoidable secondary harm.

root_risks.name: Narrative Absolutism
root_risks.definition: One-sided storytelling hardens into moral certainty without sufficient self-examination.

root_risks.name: Child Instrumentalization
root_risks.definition: Children are turned into emotional containers, allies, proof, leverage, or compensation.

root_risks.name: Dignity Collapse
root_risks.definition: One or both parties are spoken of or handled as less than fully human.

root_risks.name: Administrative Cruelty
root_risks.definition: Logistics, law, or money are used as a disguised delivery system for punishment.

root_risks.name: Self-Annihilating Guilt
root_risks.definition: Responsibility mutates into a total denial of one's own worth or right to continue.
